description.p: A local-first GTD task manager, Mindwtr helps you capture tasks quickly, organize projects and areas, and stay on top of your commitments without locking your data into a proprietary cloud.

ul.li: Fast inbox capture for tasks, notes, and ideas
Projects, areas, sections, tags, and contexts to organize your work
Daily and weekly review workflows to keep your system current
Recurring tasks, due dates, start dates, and review dates
List, board, and calendar views for different planning styles
Attachments and Markdown notes for richer task details
Focus flags, reminders, and progress tracking for active work
Offline-first storage with readable local data files
Local folder sync for simple backups and device transfers
WebDAV sync for private servers and NAS setups
Optional Dropbox and self-hosted cloud sync
Cross-platform desktop and mobile apps built around the same GTD model
Your data stays accessible without requiring an account

description.p: Mindwtr 0.7.4 adds Apple-platform iCloud sync, a desktop Obsidian workspace, stronger cross-device sync reliability, and faster mobile triage tools.

ul.li: Added native iCloud sync support on Apple platforms.
Added Obsidian vault integration on desktop with a dedicated workspace that stays hidden until enabled.
Improved sync reliability with safer merge handling, bounded cloud requests, and clearer conflict diagnostics.
Added a global mobile area switcher, time estimate filters, and daily-review focus toggles.
Improved desktop layouts, sync accessibility, and release tooling for the Node 24 transition.

description.p: Mindwtr 0.7.3 focused on more consistent sync behavior, safer local persistence, more workflow customization, and better mobile reminder handling.

ul.li: Unified desktop and mobile sync orchestration to reduce stale queued-sync edge cases.
Added date and time format overrides and opt-in inbox processing settings on desktop.
Added a visible mobile sync activity indicator and improved WebDAV rate-limit handling.
Hardened local storage, search, attachment validation, and self-hosted cloud merge behavior.
Improved notification reliability, task editing flows, and calendar permission handling.
